research_question: Track B: does a transfer-designed detector hold up leave-one-family-out across six deterministic task families? Track A: can a hierarchical competence router retain near-full verified uplift at materially fewer tool calls than tool-on-every-task, beating random and a global threshold at fixed budget?

families.prompt_template: What is {a} + {b}? Reply with the final number only.
What is {a} - {b}? Reply with the final number only.
What is {a} * {b}? Reply with the final number only.
What is {a} / {b}? Reply with the final number only.
families.construction: draw quotient q and divisor b from stratum ranges; a = q*b so division is exact; answer is q

families.prompt_template: What is ({a} * {b}) mod {m}? Reply with the final number only.

allocation_note: predicted-mixed families oversampled (320 vs 192) per protocol; regimes are predictions, measured not assumed; per-stratum n = family n / 4

generation.algorithm: per family x stratum, draw operand tuples from random.Random(seed + ':' + family_id + ':' + stratum_id), rejecting duplicates against (1) all tuples drawn anywhere in this campaign — (a, b) pairs are unique campaign-wide across families so shared arithmetic structure cannot leak across splits — and (2) every (a, b) tuple the committed M29-M34 manifests generate; auxiliary operands (c, m, quotient) come from the same rng after the tuple; stop before capture on exhaustion

split_assignment.rule: deterministic at draw time, stratified within family x stratum: the ordered tuples drawn for each family x stratum cell are assigned round-robin over a fixed split sequence built from the fractions below, so every cell contributes to every split and operand sets are disjoint across splits by construction
split_assignment.sequence_note: 16-slot cycle realizing the fractions exactly: D=6/16, R=3/16, B_test=3/16, A_test=4/16
split_assignment.read_discipline: D: all track-B fitting, feature/normalization/calibration and model selection. R: frozen-detector router and competence-prior fitting only. B_test and A_test: sealed, single evaluation read each, in that order; A_test opens only after detector, router, and priors are frozen and B_test is already read.
split_assignment.lofo_rule: leave-one-family-out refits the ENTIRE detector pipeline on D minus the withheld family, including normalization and family encoding; the withheld family's indicator maps to 'unknown'; evaluated on the withheld family's B_test rows

tool.input: the task's own predeclared expression, never model output
tool.verifier_conditioning: tool output passes the same verifier as model output

track_b_claim_rules.primary: LOFO only: per withheld family, transfer-detector performance on that family's sealed B_test rows; establishes robustness across these named withheld families under this generator/prompt/model/decode/telemetry regime and NOTHING beyond

classification.useful: both paired 95% success-delta lower bounds strictly above zero on A_test
classification.harmful: either upper bound strictly below zero

classification.efficient: paired 95% lower bound of (router - reference + margin) success delta strictly above zero AND paired 95% lower bound of call reduction strictly above zero on A_test

track_a_claim_rules.family_weights: uniform over families in A_test aggregate

privacy.private_artifacts: operands, prompts, outputs, per-task labels/predictions/triggers/splits stay gitignored

framing: track B is detector robustness measurement; track A is product/runtime routing evidence; no model self-correction claim; no training dataset from tool outputs
